purpose: Additional candidate pool for later HEI canonical batches while terminal access is unavailable. These candidates are not canonical records yet. They require final duplicate checks and public-quality staging packages before append.

status_legend.A: Likely HEI-addable after one more source/duplicate pass
status_legend.B: Potential event/update candidate, usually active entity or service-level incident
status_legend.C: Hold / weak / likely non-HEI unless better evidence appears

candidates.headline: Japanese exchange DMM Bitcoin ended service on 2025-03-08 and transferred customer accounts/assets to SBI VC Trade after the 2024 BTC theft.
candidates.hei_relevance: Strong HEI dead-side candidate. It has official Japanese service-end and SBI transfer pages plus secondary coverage linking the closure/liquidation path to the May 2024 hack.

duplicate_check.result: no existing repository hit via connector search

candidates.next_action: Stage as dead / hack with events for hack, transfer agreement, transfer completed, and service ended. Death date should likely be 2025-03-08.

candidates.headline: Turkish exchange BtcTurk suspended crypto deposits and withdrawals in August 2025 after about USD 48-49 million in suspicious hot-wallet outflows.
candidates.hei_relevance: Good active-side CEX incident candidate: hack/suspicious outflow, temporary deposits/withdrawals suspension, trading and local-currency operations reportedly continued. Not dead.

duplicate_check.result: no existing repository hit via connector search

candidates.next_action: Stage as active CEX with hack/suspicious_outflow and withdrawal_suspended events. Add restoration event only if a reliable reopening notice is found.

candidates.headline: Hyperliquid delisted JELLY/JELLYJELLY perps and forcibly settled/closed positions after a whale manipulation incident involving HLP vault losses.
candidates.hei_relevance: Useful active-side perp DEX incident candidate. It is not a closure, but it records market manipulation, forced settlement, delisting, and reimbursement/settlement actions on a major perp DEX.

duplicate_check.result: no existing repository hit via connector search

candidates.next_action: Stage only if HEI wants active DEX market-structure incidents. Use active status with events such as trading_halted/other; avoid dead-side classification.

candidates.headline: GMX v1 suffered a July 2025 approximately USD 42 million GLP liquidity-pool exploit; GMX disabled v1 trading and GLP mint/redeem on Arbitrum and Avalanche, while GMX v2 was not affected.
candidates.hei_relevance: Strong active-side DEX incident candidate because it directly affected a major decentralized perpetual exchange, halted v1 trading, and created a material protocol-state event. Not dead because v2 and the broader protocol continued.

duplicate_check.result: not checked via connector in this addendum; perform final duplicate search before staging

candidates.next_action: Run final repo duplicate search, then stage as active DEX with exploit and trading_halted events; notes should clarify that v1/GLP was affected while v2 continued.

candidates.headline: BingX reported a September 2024 hot-wallet incident and said it restored major-asset withdrawals within 24 hours, with later wallet upgrade/maintenance notices.
candidates.hei_relevance: Possible active-side CEX incident candidate. It is older and partially restoration-focused, but official BingX source quality is good. Lower priority than DMM, BtcTurk, and GMX.

duplicate_check.result: no existing repository hit via connector search

candidates.next_action: Hold unless more active-side CEX incident coverage is desired. If staged, use active with hack and withdrawal_suspended/restored events.

next_batch_recommendation.reason: DMM Bitcoin is a strong dead-side CEX record, BtcTurk is a strong active-side CEX hack/suspension record, and GMX is a strong active-side DEX exploit/trading-halt record. Hyperliquid is valuable but not a closure; BingX is valid but lower priority and older.
